user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Daily automation: Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected. Account: Quantum Analytics.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-09 15:14 EST
Subject: escalation triage - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 15:09 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-22 09:57 EST
Subject: Re: Re: escalation triage Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with escalation triage. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Quantum Analytics
Date: 2024-03-26
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), Kevin (Quantum Analytics)

[00:02:00] Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on escalation triage from our last session.

[00:05:00] Kevin (Quantum Analytics): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:07:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] Kevin (Quantum Analytics): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] Robert Foster (Quantum Analytics): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:20:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Quantum Analytics: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF32-O trap (Confuses meeting series instance with new meeting); produces complete Pre-pack brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF32-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF32-O failure mode (Confuses meeting series instance with new meeting)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF32-O -- Confuses meeting series instance with new meeting
